title: Run Effect in Node
subtitle: A script in the console — explained line by line

scenes.narration: Let's write a basic **Effect** program and run it as a Node script — no browser.
scenes.say: Let's write a basic Effect program and run it as a Node script in the console. No browser this time. And I'll explain every line.
We import Effect and pipe from the effect package, which we will install from npm in a moment.

scenes.narration: `pipe` feeds a value left-to-right through functions.
scenes.say: pipe feeds a value left to right through a series of functions. We open it here.
scenes.cue: feeds a value left to right

scenes.say: Effect.succeed of twenty-one describes a computation that succeeds with twenty-one. It only describes — nothing runs yet.
Effect.map transforms the success value. Here we double it, turning twenty-one into forty-two.

scenes.narration: `program` is just a **description** — nothing has run.
scenes.say: We close the pipe. program is just a description of what to do; nothing has executed.

scenes.narration: `Effect.runSync` executes it and returns the value.
scenes.say: Effect.runSync executes the description, synchronously, and gives us back the value.
And we print the result to the console with console dot log.

scenes.narration: Forget `runSync` and you log the **description**, not 42.
scenes.say: A quick gotcha: if you forget runSync and log program directly, you print the Effect description object, not forty-two. Effect is lazy, so you must run it.

action.replace:
import { Effect, pipe } from "effect"

const program = pipe(
  Effect.succeed(21),
  Effect.map((n) => n * 2),
)

const result = program
console.log("result =", result)

diagnostics.message: An Effect is a lazy description, not a value. Execute it with Effect.runSync(program).

scenes.say: So we keep Effect.runSync, and result holds the real number.

scenes.narration: Install Effect, then run it with Node — the console prints **result = 42**.
scenes.say: Now to the console. We install effect from npm, then run the script with node. It prints result equals forty-two.
